key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays plasticity loss, but does not eliminate it, and the loss still appears after enough training.
engineering_relevance: For continual learning systems, simply increasing model size is not a complete solution; training strategy and adaptation mechanisms still matter for long-term robustness.
method: Train GPT-style Transformer LLMs from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters
Use a multilingual continual learning setup with a held-out Vietnamese probing task
Compare plasticity under both continual and stationary multilingual training
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deteriorating performance on a held-out Vietnamese probing task, with plasticity loss appearing across all tested model sizes and following a sublinear scaling law.
watch_item: Parameter scaling may postpone observable plasticity loss, but larger models alone are unlikely to prevent eventual adaptation failure in long training runs.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
